content: Emmanuel's career promises to Tolu: 'I will graduate with distinction. I will get a high paying Job. And I will make a positive impact in the world.' He is passionate about adapting to rapid changes in technology, setting trends rather than following them. He promises to be at the pinnacle where innovation is happening. He wants to produce well-known algorithms and solve unsolvable problems. Through him, Christ will be exalted and glorified in the realm of computer science.

Emmanuel loves challenges, coding, and solving problems. He describes himself as a disagreeable person whom God has made more agreeable. He likes to debate and challenge cliché talking points. He likes to teach and simplify complex ideas. He's genuinely interested in how people think, what they think, and why they think. He wants to learn how to dance and is interested in comedy.

Tolu's favorite foods: fried potatoes, plantain chips, amala and ewedu. She likes coconut water, candy, chocolate, Schweppes, malt. Her mom made Irish potatoes for her the night she died. She loves poutine with melted cheese and bacon bits. She enjoys Japanese, Vietnamese, Thai, Korean, and Italian cuisine. She likes Opa (Greek wraps, pita bread, calamari and salad), cilantros, and Syrian food. Daily eats include seafood, coconut rice, and takoyaki balls. She eats turkey, beef, and chicken. She cooks as a hobby and cooked for therapy. Does not like KFC.
Tolu's favorite places: Her room (she likes her room), high solitary places (quiet intimate places), and her aunt's place in New Jersey.

Tolu's favorite movies: Infinity War, Whiplash, Interstellar. She loves anime/manga: Akatsuki no Yona, 'To the Alpha King' — she really likes wolves. Her favorite book is Sun Ken Rock by Sarah J Maas. The loudest Emmanuel ever heard her scream was when she was talking books with Tammy. She loves reading, writing (working on a book called 'Rafah' with an accompanying art show), and seeing libraries. The Throne of Glass series by Sarah J Maas is also a favorite.

Things Emmanuel loves about Tolu: She's healthy and not toxic. She loves the Word with her soul, heart and mind. She's visionary, a go-getter and hard-working. She's witty, creative, smart and easy to get along with. She's very pretty and he finds her very attractive. She is patient, kind and understanding. Her smile is welcoming, contagious, calming and freeing. She's emotionally intelligent. She can think logically in any scenario. She takes feedback and correction well, is very teachable and listens. He enjoys her company and she enjoys his. She deeply loves him for who he is. She has a sense of humor. She's a good cook. She's merciful, forgiving and kind. Marriage is not about Tribe, Culture, Religion, but of Character — and she is a person of outstanding character.
Tolu's love language: Being understood and believed. People being present and listening to her. These are the things she values most in how love is expressed to her.

Things Tolu likes: Live music, art exhibitions, craft, museums, theatre, reading, writing, seeing libraries, amusement parks, dressing up, beautiful things (old architecture, modern architecture with a twist), road trips, and serving the Lord.

Tolu's wishlist: A watch, a jacket, AirPods, tech haul, a better keyboard, green scenery, water lilies (very nostalgic and likes the scents), daily wear jewelry (necklace, anklet, waist beads), phone or iPad stand, one more night gown, new shorts, gym wear, monochrome wear, a dress, and a smart bulb.

Emmanuel's nicknames for Tolu: Ife mi, Love, Baby girl, Babe, Baby, Hot T, Ezar, Miracle, Fanta, My T, Tolu, T, Tolu fine, Half fine half mine, Tolu Love, My Prophet, My perfect gift, My pretty potato, My Jolly Jollof, Debby Dexterous, Sunshine, Asanwa, QT, Pink potato, Prophetic Foodie, Petite Princess, Safe Place, My heartbeat, Mine, My whole world, My golden flower, Adunni mi, My rib, Tolu mi, My prophetic princess, My answered prayer, My maiden, Dearest, Your highness, My darling, Sweeting, The song my heart sings. Note: 'Boo' is expired.

In September 2025, Emmanuel asked Tolu out. The cold winter clouds had long swallowed the summer sun, yet his heart melted like butter in her light of love. She panned away shyly, rubbing her hair gently. Then she gazed into him, 'consuming all six of my senses (yes, six… including my nonsense).' Sitting under his cold blanket in that freezing basement, he heard the warmest words: 'I'm falling in love with you.' She said it so softly, yet it shook his entire world. His heart was running a full marathon inside his chest. She said yes on September 13. He had asked her via a coded website after delivering a sermon on Proverbs 18:22.

From Tolu's journal: On August 18, 2025, Emmanuel told her he loved her through a sermon he prepared. He preached on Proverbs 18:22 ('He who finds a wife finds a good thing') and at the third point asked her directly: 'Tolu, my best friend for many years, I've fallen deeply in love with you. Would you like to take this to the next level?' She went from shock, to being flattered, to being terrified. She prayed about it. The Holy Spirit told her on September 1st: 'Tolu it's him and I can answer with all the confirmations you need but you can fall trusting me to protect your heart.' On September 11, she told him she was falling in love with him. On September 12-13, he asked her out again via the coded website and she clicked yes immediately.

In October 2025, Tolu took the relationship to the next gear — she said 'yes; he is mine and I am his.' She read an entire book about dating intelligently. Their first major storm hit: Emmanuel couldn't travel to see her, and it hurt them both deeply. But through that storm she showed her true character: supportive, empathetic, encouraging, understanding, loving, honest, enduring, calm, forgiving, comforting, patient, and believing. She became his safe harbor. Praying together was Emmanuel's favorite activity of October. Her 24th birthday was celebrated on October 26 with prayers, letters, and gifts.

In November 2025, Emmanuel was loved — not just in words, but in action and in prayer. He had mountains of schoolwork and exams, yet Tolu stayed right there offering comfort, encouragement, and every second he needed. She became his perfect comrade. Then she gave him the most dreamy birthday (November 14) any man could ever ask for. The prayers she showered over him still fuel him today. She gifted him: a jacket that carried him through winter, stylish boots, a beige turtleneck that made him feel like a runway model, and a chain that reminds him he's her star and light. Her love was audible in all 5 love languages. She also surprised him with a 50+ page love journal she'd been writing since August.

In December 2025, life got hard again but Tolu loved Emmanuel even harder. When his best friend (Brian) lost his mom, she became his quiet anchor — not with loud words but with steady prayers and the kind of presence that says 'I've got you.' School threw deadlines and exams, yet she stayed interceding for him. She turned his cold grey December into the warmest, most colorful season. On New Year's Eve, they crossed into 2026 together — long distance but hearts closer than ever. They prayed the year in over the phone, eyes locked on the screen. They talked about dreams, the babies they'll raise, the home they'll build, the legacy they'll leave. She looked straight into his eyes through the screen, smiled that smile, and said: 'This is our year.'

In January 2026, they started the year completely wrapped in each other — body, soul, and spirit, even with miles between them. Every single morning they woke up to prayer together over the phone, voices rising as one. Every late-night conversation stretched deep into the early hours as they dove into Scriptures. They made covenant declarations: to love each other louder than the distance could ever shout, to chase God harder than they chase anything else, and to keep choosing each other every single day. Emmanuel looked at her through video call one night, her smile glowing softer than any lamp, thinking, 'This is what steady, holy love feels like… even from thousands of miles away.'

In February 2026, Emmanuel finally had Tolu in his arms — flesh to flesh, heart to heart. He got to meet her in person for the first time since they fell in love. Her hands were pretty, even prettier resting in his. Her eyes were like divine pearls. Her forehead was his 'lip balm for days.' Her smile is 100x more powerful in person — it pierces through any dark cloud. He describes her food (especially the ogbono) as making him ready to marry her right there. She surprised him with a 100-page book of love and read it to him. She showed him off to friends and church family. Simple joys: seeing her in his sweatshirts, eating breakfast together, cooking side by side, watching movies cuddled up, bus rides holding hands, naps tangled up, night walks, grocery shopping like an old married pair.

In March 2026, Tolu was making Emmanuel incredibly proud. Watching her work diligently at Resilia, at her job, at Rapha, while juggling school, friends, family, and still loving him well. He calls her his 'supa woman with 3-D toned shoulders.' She moves with grace, power, and purpose. She has been his greatest inspiration. March was the month where he stood back and whispered, 'This is the woman I'm marrying.' He ended it on his knees in gratitude, thanking God for the privilege of calling her his. The 180 Days anniversary letter was written on March 12-14, 2026.

From Tolu's journal in October 2025: On October 1, she wrote about Emmanuel being her type — 'Your heart is my type. Your person is my type. I love your beautiful heart that's given to loving the Lord and loving me.' On October 2, she received her first love letter and read it '5 times while squealing, giggling and kicking my feet in the air.' On October 5, they had Date Night 3 about Dating Intelligently. On October 18, their first significant challenge happened over travel plans. She wrote: 'Everything considered, we handled our first major challenge well.' She also said 'I will NEVER EVER defend my character EVER in my life. Been there, done that, got the t-shirt, and burnt it.'

From Tolu's journal in November: On November 5, she noticed 'the light has finally reached my eyes' — for the first time, her soul looked young enough, loved enough, and in love enough to glow. On November 10, she wrote about how she loves the way Emmanuel looks at her: 'You look at me like I'm something precious. You look at me like you love me.' On November 14, his birthday — she wrote an extensive birthday prayer declaring blessings, favor, and anointing over his life. She wrote Sonnet 43 (Elizabeth B. Browning) for him: 'How do I love thee? Let me count the ways.' On November 16, Gratitude Date Night — their 2 month anniversary.

From Tolu's journal in December 2025: On November 26-27, they had a date night at Brian's where they sang hymns together. Tolu had a panic attack during the hymn singing because it triggered memories from a difficult period. Emmanuel prayed for her and firmly said 'there was enough air to breathe' — which grounded her. She thought 'I'm definitely going to marry this guy.' On December 4, she had her first good dream in years — a prophetic dream about Emmanuel sending her a marriage form filled with his information. On December 7, she wrote: 'Ade mi, you are my future. You are God's gift to me.' On December 13, their 3-month anniversary. In January 2026, she wrote about how much she loves having him around and their morning prayer routine.

From Tolu's journal, February 25, 2026 — reflections on meeting Emmanuel in person: 'When you knocked on my door and I saw you, I thought: Finally… That's my babe… I know this person… I've missed you.' Hugging him felt like 'the most natural thing' and 'like coming home.' He looked so handsome and smelled so good. She describes him walking in 'like a dream come true.' She ends with: 'Miracle no dey tire Jesus.'

Emmanuel and Tolu started dating on September 13, 2025. They had been best friends for 7-8 years before that. Emmanuel confessed his love on August 18, 2025, via a sermon on Proverbs 18:22. Tolu said yes on September 13 via the coded website. Their 3-month anniversary was December 13, 2025. Their 6-month anniversary was March 13, 2026.

Their first virtual date was on September 20-21, 2025. They spent the evening getting to know each other through questions. Emmanuel documented her answers in boo.pdf: her favorite place (her room, high solitary places), she visited Berlin and never wants to return (the smell of smoke), she doesn't like KFC, she likes modern architecture. Her favorite foods are potatoes, amala and ewedu. She cooks as a hobby. Her favorite movies: Infinity War, Whiplash, Interstellar. If she could bring three things to a deserted island: Emmanuel, Bible, something to write with. Her biggest pet peeve: disrespect.

Emmanuel and Tolu were best friends for 7-8 years before dating. They are both Nigerian, Christian, and deeply connected through faith. Their friendship involved late-night talks, inside jokes, and supporting each other through life. Emmanuel fell in love first and prepared a sermon on Proverbs 18:22 to confess his feelings. He also built a coded website to ask her out. Tolu's journal reveals she was not expecting it but found she had 'no defenses, excuses or reasons why it couldn't be you.' The Holy Spirit was notably supportive of the match, according to Tolu: 'He was grinning, teasing, smiling and smirking like he knew something I didn't.'

Deep Christian faith is the foundation of Emmanuel and Tolu's relationship. Tolu is trained in the prophetic and has the gift of discernment — she sees and hears from the spirit. She was saved on August 26, 2018. Emmanuel is growing in spiritual responsibility and listening to the Holy Spirit. They pray together regularly, study scripture together, and their morning routine includes prayer over the phone. Key scriptures for them: Proverbs 18:22 (the sermon he asked her out with), 1 Corinthians 13 (love chapter), Isaiah 43, Songs of Solomon, Ecclesiastes 4:9-12 (threefold cord). They attend church communities (DLBC background, now CCI). Their shared mission involves caring for the most vulnerable and bringing the gospel to those who suffer.

Emmanuel and Tolu maintain a long-distance relationship. They stay connected through daily calls, FaceTime, prayer over the phone, watching movies together virtually, and planning future trips. They crossed New Year's Eve together on video call. Their first in-person meeting as a couple was in February 2026 in Winnipeg. Tolu's journal emphasizes: 'A long distance relationship without planning to meet is planning to fail.' They prioritize consistent connection: good morning/goodnight messages, weekly mini-dates, and weekend intentional date nights.

Activities and date ideas documented: Call siblings and friends, create a silly TikTok together, teach her how to solve a Rubik's cube, fashion fits, couples photo shoot, movie, shopping, play cards, talk about Songs of Solomon, braid hair, go shopping, form a handshake, massage feet and neck, late night walk, watch old Disney movie cuddled up, shopping at Zara, perfume shopping, watch movies together, dinner, go to an arcade, gym together, breakfast dates, bible study evenings. Virtual date ideas: parallel scrolling on FaceTime, snack dates, watch TikToks/Reels together, cook the same meal, walk-and-talk with headphones, 20 questions, Would You Rather, online games (Skribbl.io, Wordle, GeoGuessr), read poetry to each other, share songs, plan future trips.

Emmanuel's collection of pickup lines for Tolu: 'Y = mx + babe', 'If your left eye okay? You've been looking right all day', 'Sorry to bother you, I typed beautiful on Google maps and it led me here', 'Do you have a map? I just got lost in your eyes', 'I'm not even playing cards and yet I pulled a queen', 'If beauty were a crime, you'd be serving a life sentence', 'When Solomon said there's nothing new under the sun, he didn't take account of this beauty', 'I can always count on you (counting fingers)', 'Kiss me on the cheek if I'm wrong, but fish can fly, right?', 'I'm a mathematician, give me your number and I will show you how I divide and multiply later.'

How Emmanuel and Tolu define their relationship: They are inseparable. Building their future together in Christ. Peaceful. Considerate. Living like Christ. Thankful. Never having to question if the other loves them. Reassuring, very present. Supportive. Caring for each other's well-being. Making each other's lives easier.

Emmanuel's birthday prayer for Tolu's 24th birthday (October 26, 2025): A detailed prayer covering her health, mind, spirit, career, the book 'Rafah', reconciliation with her parents, emotional healing, and their relationship. Key declarations: 'You are my royal queen, deserving of love, warmth, and all the joy this world can give.' He prayed for her book Rafah and accompanying art show. He prayed for reconciliation with her parents. He prayed for emotional healing and wholeness. He prayed for himself to be molded into the protector God envisions for her.

Tolu's birthday prayer for Emmanuel's 23rd birthday (November 14, 2025): An extensive prayer declaring over his life from Isaiah 43, Psalm 91, Joshua 1, and many other scriptures. Key declarations: 'True to his name Igbagboyemi, his faith in you will never be put to shame.' 'For Ade mi, the crown who has come home. May the scepter never leave his hands.' 'He will bear responsibility with strength, wisdom and love.' She prayed for God's favour to surround him like a shield, for anointing like Elijah and Elisha, for supernatural experiences as ordinary reality.

Emmanuel's Valentine's poem for Tolu (January 13, 2026): 'In the hush between heartbeats where the world forgets its noise, I find you — like sunrise finds the ocean, soft, certain, golden. You are the quiet warmth that lingers on winter mornings, the gentle spark that turns ordinary moments into constellations. When you laugh, the air feels lighter, as if even gravity leans closer just to listen. Your eyes hold stories I want to spend a lifetime reading, page by page, word by word, never rushing the beauty of each line. And your smile — that breathtaking, luminous smile — it is my favorite place to be. If love is a journey, then every step beside you is home.' He asked: 'To the girl with the most beautiful smile, will you be my valentine?'

Emmanuel asked Tolu out by writing and delivering a full sermon titled 'He Who Finds a Wife Finds a Good Thing' based on Proverbs 18:22. The sermon had 3 points: 1) God's Design for Companionship (Genesis 2:18), 2) The Value of a Godly Relationship (1 Cor 13, Proverbs 31), and 3) Embracing the Blessing When It's Found — where he turned to Tolu directly and said: 'Tolu I truly believe God's shown me my good thing in you. Your joy lights up my world, your wisdom challenges me. You're my Proverbs 31 woman, and I'm praying you're my future. Will you go out with me, explore what God has for us, and maybe, one day, build a covenant together under His favor?' Then he sent her to the coded website where she clicked yes.

From Tolu's journal, August 23, 2025: After the AISIC program, an older man approached her, told her she had a 'royal look,' and asked if she wanted a husband. After she said yes with certainty, he told her that her husband was coming and she shouldn't go looking — when he came for her she would know. He prayed for her and gave her his card, saying to invite him to the wedding in 2-3 years. The first thing she thought about when he spoke was Emmanuel. He had the same timeline (2-3 years) that Emmanuel later independently mentioned when presenting his plans for the future.

God's message to Tolu about Emmanuel (September 1, 2025): 'Tolu, you're like God, I can do anything for you until it's time to let someone love you. In a benign way you don't think you are deserving and that bothers me. I want the best for you. Someone who seeks me for you. In many ways, especially of the heart, Emmanuel is ready. He will find as he seeks diligently for me with all his heart. Tolu it's him and I can answer with all the confirmations you need but you can fall trusting me to protect your heart the way I always have from the beginning. You are first mine before anybody else's. I do think he needs to grow up a little more but he is already on the right track. You're simply asking if it's okay to fall. I'll catch you. He will too.'

Tolu is described as a 'king maker' who covers really important people — and the repercussion of that is she is a spiritual target. Emmanuel's role is to cover her in prayer and be her spiritual protection. She has conquered 15 to 20 territories. Emmanuel needs to grow in the prophetic, always pray for her, and speak directly to the Holy Spirit. She is learning to trust and growing in spiritual responsibility. From Tolu's journal, she declared over Emmanuel: 'You will grow in your knowledge of God and the authority you have in Him. You will heal the sick, raise the dead, cast out demons, trample upon serpents and scorpions without consequence.'

From Tolu's journal (November 30 reflection): She lost her mother and grew up fast — at ten she decided not to be a burden. She learned to cook, do chores, pick up the slack, hide her feelings. When her stepmom came, she learned to give grace endlessly. She struggled with finding a will to live. She survived months of severe pain and hopelessness. Recently, she realized 'the light has finally reached my eyes' — she's finally 'light enough, hopeful enough, young enough, loved enough and in love enough.' God showed her that the lesson in every trial was for the fire: 'It's not every light you can quench, it's not every child you can touch. Tolu is made of stronger stuff.' Emmanuel's love is part of her healing. She says: 'I've always wanted someone to stay. You promise me forever with such conviction and faith that I have to believe you.'

Emmanuel visited Tolu in Winnipeg in February 2026 for their first in-person meeting as a couple. Things they did together: wore matching clothes (she loved his sweatshirts), ate breakfast together, cooked side by side, watched movies cuddled up, took bus rides holding hands, picked her up and spun her around, read together, exchanged gifts, took naps tangled up, went on quiet night walks, prayed together, hugged endlessly, rubbed each other's heads, took couple selfies, went grocery shopping, received mentorship together. He met her best friend Peace, went shopping, visited her pastor, and had their first in-person date on Valentine's Day at Ume Yummy (a Vietnamese restaurant).

Their long-distance weekly rhythm: Daily light connection (5-15 min) — good morning or good night voice note/text, one check-in question. Midweek mini-date (30-45 min, once per week) — watch a show episode, play a game, eat on FaceTime. Weekend intentional date night (60-90 min) — emotional check-in, shared activity, light affection / future talk. Optional flex slot if one had a hard week or they're missing each other more than usual.
Debates Emmanuel and Tolu enjoy: Genesis, Is psychology a science, Is feminism a net positive, Are Catholics sinners. They enjoy intellectual discussions and challenging each other's perspectives.

Emmanuel and Tolu's bucket list: Movies, Church, Vacation, Playing sports. Also: dancing (west coast swing), learning voice training, travel that expands horizons. Their future vision includes a quiet neighbourhood, reserved but present community, best schools for kids, a peaceful and joyful home. Tolu wants trees around (not a forest), high places, and a home with balconies and a really nice view.

Tolu's health details: Needs to hydrate a lot. Contacts worn for too long cause headaches. Needs breaks to rest her eyes. Very sensation-sensitive. Her biggest pet peeve is disrespect — and 'to be okay with things that are not okay (sitting in the fire and calling it warmth).'
